revision.text:
[[ja:彦太忍信命]][[zh:彦太忍信命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|spouse={{ill|Q106107899|qid=Q106107899|1=Inatsu-hime no Mikoto}}|children={{ill|Prince Yanushioshiotakeokokoro|qid=Q11465637|1=Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|mother={{ill|Ikagashikome|qid=Q10885176|1=Ikagashikome}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|1=Emperor Kaika}}; {{ill|Prince Takehaniyasuhiko|qid=Q11545865|1=Prince Takehaniyasuhiko}}; {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|qid=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Princess Mimatsuhime|qid=Q11489233|1=Princess Mimatsuhime}}; {{ill|Sujin|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}; {{ill|Yamatototohime no mikoto|qid=Q11387096|1=Yamatototohime no mikoto}}}} {{Infobox Aristocrat | name = Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = | death_date = | alias = Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto (比古布都押之信命) | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = | clan = [[Kii Clan]] (紀氏 ancestor) | parents = Father: {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}; Mother: [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]] | siblings = | spouse = [[Katsuragi Takachinabime no Mikoto]] (記), [[Kagehime|Yamashita Kagebime no Mikoto]] (記) | children = [[Amamino Sukune|Aji Uchino Sukune]] (記), [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Tatenouchi no Sukune]] (記), [[Yamatsunushi no Mikoto|Oshio no Takeokokoro no Mikoto]] (紀) | special_notes = }} '''Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto''' (彦太忍信命/比古布都押之信命, birth and death unknown) is a legendary prince mentioned in ancient Japanese texts, such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''. He is recorded as the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}} and a progenitor of the [[Kii Clan]]. == Lineage ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
Names primarily follow the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternative names from the ''Kojiki'' in parentheses. {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|02='''Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto'''
{{Small|(比古布都押之信命)}}|03=[[Yamatsunushi no Mikoto|Oshio no Takeokokoro no Mikoto]]
{{Small|(not mentioned in Kojiki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01|02| 00='''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
{{Small|(Tatenouchi no Sukune)}}|01=[[Hata Yashiro|Hata Yashiro no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Hatayashiro no Sukune)}}|02=　→　[[:en:Hata Clan|Hata Clan]]}} {{familytree/end}}
Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}} and [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]] (also known as Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto), daughter of [[Ōheshoki no Mikoto]]. He was the father or grandfather of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], a legendary statesman and hero, as well as [[Yamatsunushi no Mikoto|Oshio no Takeokokoro no Mikoto]], according to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is said to have married Yamashita Kagebime no Mikoto and fathered Tatenouchi no Sukune (Takenouchi no Sukune), and to have married Katsuragi Takachinabime no Mikoto, with whom he fathered Aji Uchino Sukune. The ''[[Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Record]]'' suggests an alternate genealogy, listing him as a son of Emperor Kaika instead. == Descendants == Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto is regarded as an ancestor of numerous clans, particularly the [[Kii Clan]], through his son [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' lists many descendants from his line, including: * [[Ishikawa Clan]] - Left Kyoto Imperial Line * [[Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi Clan]] * [[Kose Clan]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} His line is also tied to regional rulers and historical figures, such as the ''kuni no miyatsuko'' (provincial rulers) of Kii Province. == Cultural References == Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto is mentioned in shrine records, such as the genealogies of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] and [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]], as a progenitor of aristocratic families and local rulers. His legacy remains significant in the oral and written traditions of ancient Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] * [[Kii Clan]] * [[:en:Japanese Mythology|Japanese Mythology]] {{Japan-hist-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|彦太忍信命|version=93492242|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:彦太忍信命]] [[zh:彦太忍信命]] {{wikidata link|Q11487911}} [[Category:Japanese royalty]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
